description: Curated, literature-backed reference of known microbial interactions relevant to peanut (Arachis hypogaea) rhizosphere and soil systems. Used by scripts/network_analysis.py to annotate statistically detected co-occurrence edges with published evidence. Matching is done on taxon names at the configured taxonomic rank (case-insensitive substring match against either partner). Each entry records the expected direction of association so a detected correlation can be reported as CONSISTENT or DISCORDANT with the literature.
disclaimer: These entries are curated candidate references to aid biological interpretation of statistical associations. A name-level match flags an edge as literature-supported for the analyst to verify against the cited source; it is NOT automated proof that the specific ASVs interact. Always confirm against the primary reference.

expected_sign_legend.positive: taxa expected to co-occur / mutualistic / helper relationship
expected_sign_legend.negative: taxa expected to exclude one another / antagonism / biocontrol
expected_sign_legend.context: sign depends on conditions (e.g. nutrient status, stress)

interactions.mechanism: Bradyrhizobium (e.g. B. arachidis) forms determinate root nodules on peanut and fixes atmospheric N2, exchanged for plant photosynthate; abundance tracks nodulation and flavonoid signalling.

references.citation: Bacteroid Development, Transcriptome, and Symbiotic Nitrogen-Fixing Comparison of Bradyrhizobium arachidis in Nodules of Peanut (Arachis hypogaea)
Maize and peanut intercropping improves nitrogen accumulation by promoting flavonoids and Bradyrhizobium abundance in rhizosphere. Front. Plant Sci. 2022

interactions.mechanism: AMF (Glomeromycota) colonise peanut roots, extend hyphal networks that improve phosphorus/water uptake and alleviate salinity/drought stress; reshapes rhizosphere microbiome and increases yield.

references.citation: Arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi regulate the peanut rhizosphere microbiome to alleviate salinity stress and enhance yield. Front. Microbiol. 2025
The multifaceted roles of Arbuscular Mycorrhizal Fungi in peanut responses to salt, drought, and cold stress

interactions.mechanism: AMF hyphae positively associate with helper bacteria such as Pseudomonas, Rhizobium and Streptomyces that promote spore germination, hyphal growth and nutrient mobilisation in the hyphosphere.

references.citation: Nutrient-dependent cross-kingdom interactions in the hyphosphere of an arbuscular mycorrhizal fungus. ISME/PMC 2024
Co-occurrence networks reveal candidate AMF-microbe assemblages for generalist and crop-specific inocula. Mycorrhiza 2026

interactions.mechanism: Actinobacteria (Streptomyces) frequently co-occur with AMF and can enhance mycorrhization and suppress soil-borne pathogens via antibiotic production.

references.citation: Long-Term Persistence of Arbuscular Mycorrhizal Fungi and Their Networks of Co-occurring Microbes. PMC8914178

interactions.mechanism: Trichoderma species antagonise Aspergillus flavus (aflatoxin producer on peanut) through mycoparasitism, competition and antifungal metabolites, reducing pre-harvest aflatoxin contamination.

references.citation: Biocontrol of Aspergillus flavus and aflatoxin in peanut by antagonistic Trichoderma spp. (review literature)

interactions.mechanism: Bacillus spp. (e.g. B. subtilis, B. velezensis) inhibit Aspergillus flavus growth and aflatoxin biosynthesis via lipopeptides (iturin, fengycin, surfactin) and competition; used as peanut biocontrol inoculants.

references.citation: Peanut challenged with fungal pathogens shows induced systemic resistance when co-inoculated with Bacillus sp. CHEP5 and Bradyrhizobium sp.

interactions.mechanism: Fluorescent Pseudomonas suppress Fusarium and other soil-borne pathogens through siderophore-mediated iron competition, 2,4-DAPG and other antibiotics, and induced systemic resistance.

references.citation: Fluorescent Pseudomonas as biocontrol of Fusarium wilt (classical PGPR mechanism literature)

interactions.mechanism: Bacillus and Pseudomonas are dominant PGPR guilds that can co-occur and synergise (nutrient solubilisation, ISR) but may also compete for niche/resources depending on carbon availability.

references.citation: Legume rhizodeposition promotes nitrogen fixation by soil microbiota under crop diversification. Nat. Commun. 2024

interactions.mechanism: Root-associated beneficial fungi (e.g. Serendipita) positively associate with N-fixing and PGPR bacteria in co-occurrence networks, indicating cooperative rhizosphere assembly.

references.citation: AMF-microbe co-occurrence networks show positive associations among Rhizobium, Pseudomonas, Streptomyces and Serendipita. Mycorrhiza 2026

method_references.citation: Friedman J, Alm EJ (2012) Inferring Correlation Networks from Genomic Survey Data. PLoS Comput Biol 8(9):e1002687
Benjamini Y, Hochberg Y (1995) Controlling the False Discovery Rate. J R Stat Soc B 57(1):289-300

method_references.topic: Pitfalls of network inference on compositional data
method_references.citation: Difficulty in inferring microbial community structure based on co-occurrence network approaches. BMC Bioinformatics 2019
